failure_summary: Critical failures across all criteria. First, 0/9 posts pass the 9/7 floor—all have 'passes_9_7_floor': false with empty scores, indicating the scoring pipeline failed or was bypassed. Second, anchor concentration is catastrophic: all 9 posts use the SAME anchor ('Sitting through dozens of enterprise sales calls, watching SE introductions go wrong in real time'), violating the 5+ distinct anchors requirement by extreme margin. Third, argument convergence is total: all posts argue the identical thesis—SEs fail because orgs structurally disempower them, and SEs complicitly accept this. The 'two failures leading to the same outcome' closer appears verbatim or near-verbatim in every post. Fourth, mechanic distribution is monolithic: all use pattern_observation opener with identical 'X hires in Y years, same result' structure. The pack is essentially one post cloned 9 times with minor lexical variation (A1-A4: 'five SE leads/SEs in three/two years'; B1-B5: alternate openers but same body structure). Register is consistent but the repetition creates diminishing returns—by B3-B5 the 'technical resource' framing becomes predictable. The A2 attempt to inject MUFG anchor (A011) was abandoned or failed validation. No tier-1 anchors from inventory (A001-A013) were utilized despite availability. The pack reads as a single template with synonym substitution, not diverse explorations of a theme.
recommendations: Halt generation and debug scoring pipeline—empty scores with false floor flags suggest validation system failure
Mandate minimum 5 distinct anchors from tier-1 inventory; current single-anchor dependency creates unshippable concentration
Enforce argument variation: require at least 3 distinct thesis angles (e.g., SE complicity only, org structure only, buyer psychology only, solution path forward)
Diversify opener mechanics: deploy scene_entry (A009's '$500K deal' quoted dialogue), specific_number_opener (A006's market data inversion), or confession/frame_break
Consider splitting into 3 thematic packs of 3 posts each rather than 9 variants of identical structure
Review iteration 1 'ship' decision in rejection history—appears to have been overridden or erroneous given current state
